title: Long IPA marks
explanation: The ː mark means the sound is held longer. Vietnamese hints often double the vowel, such as ii or uu.

title: Stress mark
explanation: The ˈ mark shows the next syllable is stronger. The Vietnamese hint uses hyphens to make chunks easier to see.

title: Schwa is weak
explanation: /ə/ is the most common weak English vowel. Vietnamese learners can think of it as a very light 'ơ'.

title: Two TH sounds
explanation: /θ/ has no voice, as in think. /ð/ has voice vibration, as in this.

title: Final NG
explanation: /ŋ/ is made at the back of the mouth. Avoid adding a strong g after it unless the IPA shows /ɡ/.
